name: scenario-2-fix-fails
description: Same alert as scenario-1, but with a hand-crafted bad patch (timeout set to 5000ms — too long to trip the actual 1.5s tail). Verifier should REJECT. This is the scenario that proves the verifier is willing to disagree with the AI-SRE.

alert.title: [p99 high] checkout latency above SLO
alert.event_msg: Monitor 'checkout.p99.latency' triggered. p99 over the last 10 minutes is 1,420ms; SLO is 500ms.
alert.body: Monitor 'checkout.p99.latency' triggered. p99 over the last 10 minutes is 1,420ms; SLO is 500ms.

prepared_patch.summary: Add 5000ms timeout to fetch_price (too generous — won't trip the actual tail).
prepared_patch.hypothesis: The pricing call is occasionally slow. Adding a timeout will bound the worst case.

files.before:
"""Checkout endpoint.

PLANTED BUG: `fetch_price` is called with no client-side timeout. The pricing
service has a 10% tail-latency profile (~1.5s per affected call), so any
request that lands on a tail call inherits the full 1.5s wait. With even
modest traffic, p99 latency drifts well above SLO and the alert fires.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from .pricing import fetch_price


def checkout(item_id: str, qty: int) -> dict:
    # BUG: no timeout on the downstream pricing call.
    price = fetch_price(item_id)
    return {"item_id": item_id, "qty": qty, "total": price * qty}
